StarryUniGraph
  • Creating Temporal GNN Models
  • View page source

Creating Temporal GNN Models

1. Continuous-time Temporal GNN Models

To create a continuous-time temporal GNN model, we first need to define a configuration file with the suffix yml to specify the model structures and parameters. Here we use the configuration file TGN.yml for TGN model as an example:

sampling:
  - layer: 1
    neighbor:
      - 10
    strategy: 'recent'
    prop_time: False
    history: 1
    duration: 0
    num_thread: 32
memory:
  - type: 'node'
    dim_time: 100
    deliver_to: 'self'
    mail_combine: 'last'
    memory_update: 'gru'
    mailbox_size: 1
    combine_node_feature: True
    dim_out: 100
gnn:
  - arch: 'transformer_attention'
    use_src_emb: False
    use_dst_emb: False
    layer: 1
    att_head: 2
    dim_time: 100
    dim_out: 100
train:
  - epoch: 20
    batch_size: 200
    # reorder: 16
    lr: 0.0001
    dropout: 0.2
    att_dropout: 0.2
    all_on_gpu: True

The configuration file is composed of four parts: sampling, memory, gnn and train. Here are their meanings:

  • sampling: This part specifies the sampling strategy for the temporal graph. layer field specifies the number of layers in the sampling strategy. The neighbor field specifies the number of neighbors to sample for each layer. The strategy field specifies the sampling strategy(recent or uniform). The prop_time field specifies whether to propagate the time information. The history field specifies the number of historical timestamps to use. The duration field specifies the duration of the time window. The num_thread field specifies the number of threads to use for sampling.

  • memory: This part specifies the memory module. type field specifies the type of memory module(node or none). dim_time field specifies the dimension of the time embedding. deliver_to field specifies the destination of the message. mail_combine field specifies the way to combine the messages. memory_update field specifies the way to update the memory. mailbox_size field specifies the size of the mailbox. combine_node_feature field specifies whether to combine the node features. dim_out field specifies the dimension of the output.

  • gnn: This part specifies the GNN module. arch field specifies the architecture of the GNN module. use_src_emb field specifies whether to use the source embedding. use_dst_emb field specifies whether to use the destination embedding. layer field specifies the number of layers in the GNN module. att_head field specifies the number of attention heads. dim_time field specifies the dimension of the time embedding. dim_out field specifies the dimension of the output.

  • train: This part specifies the training parameters. epoch field specifies the number of epochs. batch_size field specifies the batch size. lr field specifies the learning rate. dropout field specifies the dropout rate. att_dropout field specifies the attention dropout rate. all_on_gpu field specifies whether to put all the data on GPU.

After defining the configuration file, we can firstly read the parameters from the configuration file and create the model by constructing a General Model object:

def parse_config(f):
    conf = yaml.safe_load(open(f, 'r'))
    sample_param = conf['sampling'][0]
    memory_param = conf['memory'][0]
    gnn_param = conf['gnn'][0]
    train_param = conf['train'][0]
    return sample_param, memory_param, gnn_param, train_param

sample_param, memory_param, gnn_param, train_param = parse_config('./config/{}.yml'.format(args.model))
model = GeneralModel(gnn_dim_node, gnn_dim_edge, sample_param, memory_param, gnn_param, train_param).cuda()
model = DDP(model)

Then a GeneralModel object is created. If needed, we can adjust the model’s parameters by modifying the contents of the configuration file. Here we provide 5 models for continuous-time temporal GNNs:

  • TGN: The TGN model proposed in Temporal Graph Networks for Deep Learning on Dynamic Graphs.

  • DyRep: The DyRep model proposed in Representation Learning and Reasoning on Temporal Knowledge Graphs.

  • TIGER: The TIGER model proposed in TIGER: A Transformer-Based Framework for Temporal Knowledge Graph Completion.

  • Jodie: The Jodie model proposed in JODIE: Joint Optimization of Dynamics and Importance for Online Embedding.

  • TGAT: The TGAT model proposed in Temporal Graph Attention for Deep Temporal Modeling.

2. Discrete-time Temporal GNN Models

To create a discrete-time temporal GNN model, we first need to define a configuration file with the suffix yml to specify the model structures and parameters. Here we use the configuration file parameters_elliptic_egcn_o.yaml for egcn_o model as an example:

dataset_args:
    data: elliptic_temporal
    elliptic_args:
      folder: ./data/elliptic_temporal
      tar_file: elliptic_bitcoin_dataset_cont.tar.gz
      feats_file: elliptic_bitcoin_dataset_cont/elliptic_txs_features.csv
      edges_file: elliptic_bitcoin_dataset_cont/elliptic_txs_edgelist_timed.csv
      classes_file: elliptic_bitcoin_dataset_cont/elliptic_txs_classes.csv
      times_file: elliptic_bitcoin_dataset_cont/elliptic_txs_nodetime.csv
      aggr_time: 1

train:
    use_cuda: True
    use_logfile: True
    model: egcn_o
    task: node_cls

    class_weights: [ 0.35, 0.65]
    use_2_hot_node_feats: False
    use_1_hot_node_feats: False
    save_node_embeddings: True

    train_proportion: 0.65
    dev_proportion: 0.1
    num_epochs: 800
    steps_accum_gradients: 1
    learning_rate: 0.001
    learning_rate_min: 0.001
    learning_rate_max: 0.02
    negative_mult_training: 20
    negative_mult_test: 100
    smart_neg_sampling: False
    seed: 1234
    target_measure: F1
    target_class: 1
    early_stop_patience: 100

    eval_after_epochs: 5
    adj_mat_time_window: 1
    adj_mat_time_window_min: 1
    adj_mat_time_window_max: 10
    num_hist_steps: 5 # number of previous steps used for prediction
    num_hist_steps_min: 3 # only used if num_hist_steps: None
    num_hist_steps_max: 10 # only used if num_hist_steps: None
    data_loading_params:
      batch_size: 1
      num_workers: 6

gcn_parameters:
  feats_per_node: 50
  feats_per_node_min: 30
  feats_per_node_max: 312
  layer_1_feats: 256
  layer_1_feats_min: 30
  layer_1_feats_max: 500
  layer_2_feats: None
  layer_2_feats_same_as_l1: True
  k_top_grcu: 200
  num_layers: 2
  lstm_l1_layers: 125
  lstm_l1_feats: 100
  lstm_l1_feats_min: 50
  lstm_l1_feats_max: 500
  lstm_l2_layers: 1
  lstm_l2_feats: 400
  lstm_l2_feats_same_as_l1: True
  cls_feats: 307
  cls_feats_min: 100
  cls_feats_max: 700

The configuration file is composed of three parts: dataset_args, train and gcn_parameters. Here are their meanings:

  • dataset_args: This part specifies some configurations for the dataset used. data specifies the name of the dataset. elliptic_args contains parameters related to the dataset, including the folder location of the dataset, the name of the data file, and so on.

  • train: This part specifies the training parameters. use_cuda indicates whether cuda is used for computation. use_logfile indicates whether log files are used to record running processes. model indicates the model name to use. task indicates the type of the task. class_weights is the class weight, which deals with class imbalance. use_2_hot_node_feats and use_2_hot_node_feats indicates whether one-hot encoding is used. save_node_embeddings indicates whether the node embedding is saved. train_proportion and dev_proportion indicates the ratio of training set and validation set. num_epochs indicates the total number of rounds of training. steps_accum_gradients indicates the number of steps for gradient accumulation, which is used to implement gradient accumulation. learning_rate indicates learning rate. negative_mult_training and negative_mult_test indicates the multiple of negative sampling at training and test time. smart_neg_sampling indicates whether to use negative-only sampling. seed indicates the random number seed. target_measure and target_class denote the target evaluation metric and target category respectively. early_stop_patience is the patience value of early stopping, stopping the training if the performance on the validation set does not improve within a certain number of rounds. eval_after_epochs indicates how many rounds the evaluation should be performed. adj_mat_time_window indicates the time window of the adjacency matrix. num_hist_steps indicates the number of historical steps used for prediction. data_loading_params indicates data loading parameters, including batch size and number of worker threads.

  • gcn_parameters: This part specifies the GCN module.These include the number of features per node, the number of features per layer, and the parameters of the LSTM layer. Notice that there are parameters with the suffixes min and max, which means that the parameter values will be randomly generated between min and max based on the random number seed.

After defining the configuration file, we can firstly read the parameters from the configuration file and create a GNN model that supports partition parallelism and use it for later training:

def create_parser():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(formatter_class=argparse.RawTextHelpFormatter)
    parser.add_argument('--config_file', default='experiments/parameters_elliptic_egcn_o.yaml', type=argparse.FileType(mode='r'), help='optional, yaml file containing parameters to be used, overrides command line parameters')
    parser.add_argument('--gpu', default=0, type=int, help='gpu id')

    return parser

def parse_args(parser):
    args = parser.parse_args()
    if args.config_file:
        data = yaml.load(args.config_file, Loader=yaml.FullLoader)
        delattr(args, 'config_file')
        arg_dict = args.__dict__
        for key, value in data.items():
            arg_dict[key] = value
    if args.model in ['dysat', 'gcrn']:
        return args

    args.learning_rate = random_param_value(args.learning_rate, args.learning_rate_min, args.learning_rate_max, type='logscale')
    args.num_hist_steps = random_param_value(args.num_hist_steps, args.num_hist_steps_min, args.num_hist_steps_max, type='int')
    args.gcn_parameters['feats_per_node'] = random_param_value(args.gcn_parameters['feats_per_node'], args.gcn_parameters['feats_per_node_min'], args.gcn_parameters['feats_per_node_max'], type='int')
    args.gcn_parameters['layer_1_feats'] = random_param_value(args.gcn_parameters['layer_1_feats'], args.gcn_parameters['layer_1_feats_min'], args.gcn_parameters['layer_1_feats_max'], type='int')
    if args.gcn_parameters['layer_2_feats_same_as_l1'] or args.gcn_parameters['layer_2_feats_same_as_l1'].lower()=='true':
        args.gcn_parameters['layer_2_feats'] = args.gcn_parameters['layer_1_feats']
    else:
        args.gcn_parameters['layer_2_feats'] = random_param_value(args.gcn_parameters['layer_2_feats'], args.gcn_parameters['layer_1_feats_min'], args.gcn_parameters['layer_1_feats_max'], type='int')
    args.gcn_parameters['lstm_l1_feats'] = random_param_value(args.gcn_parameters['lstm_l1_feats'], args.gcn_parameters['lstm_l1_feats_min'], args.gcn_parameters['lstm_l1_feats_max'], type='int')
    if args.gcn_parameters['lstm_l2_feats_same_as_l1'] or args.gcn_parameters['lstm_l2_feats_same_as_l1'].lower()=='true':
        args.gcn_parameters['lstm_l2_feats'] = args.gcn_parameters['lstm_l1_feats']
    else:
        args.gcn_parameters['lstm_l2_feats'] = random_param_value(args.gcn_parameters['lstm_l2_feats'], args.gcn_parameters['lstm_l1_feats_min'], args.gcn_parameters['lstm_l1_feats_max'], type='int')
    args.gcn_parameters['cls_feats'] = random_param_value(args.gcn_parameters['cls_feats'], args.gcn_parameters['cls_feats_min'], args.gcn_parameters['cls_feats_max'], type='int')

    return args

    parser = u.create_parser()
    args = u.parse_args(parser)

    sync_gnn = build_model(args, graph=g, group=group)

Then a build_model object is created. If needed, we can adjust the model’s parameters by modifying the contents of the configuration file. Here we provide 4 models for discrete-time temporal GNNs:

  • EloveGCN: The EGCN model proposed in Evolving graph convolutional networks for dynamic graphs.

  • DySAT: The DySAT model proposed in Deep neural representation learning on dynamic graphs via self-attention networks.

  • GCRN: The GCRN model proposed in Structured sequence modeling with graph convolutional recurrent networks.

  • TGCN: The TGCN model proposed in Tag graph convolutional network for tag-aware recommendation.
